name: artifacts
context: Upload and download GitHub Actions build artifacts via actions/upload-artifact, actions/download-artifact, and the REST API
info: Manage build artifacts produced by GitHub Actions workflow runs. Upload artifacts (build outputs, test reports, coverage data) from workflow steps using `actions/upload-artifact@v4`. Download artifacts in subsequent jobs or via the REST API and `gh` CLI. Artifacts are retained for 90 days by default (configurable). Use artifacts to pass data between workflow jobs, preserve build outputs for deployment, and store test results for analysis. See [[github/actions/workflows]] for defining workflows and [[github/actions/runs]] for run management.
body:
# Build Artifacts

Artifacts let you persist data from workflow runs — build outputs, test reports, logs, and any files you need to share between jobs or download later.

## Upload Artifacts in Workflows

```yaml
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci && npm run build

      # Upload build output
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: dist/
          retention-days: 30

      # Upload test reports
      - run: npm test -- --reporter=junit --output=test-results.xml
        continue-on-error: true
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()  # Upload even if tests fail
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: test-results.xml

      # Upload multiple paths
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: reports
          path: |
            coverage/
            test-results.xml
            *.log
          if-no-files-found: warn  # warn, error, or ignore
```

## Download Artifacts Between Jobs

```yaml
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci && npm run build
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: dist/

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      # Download artifact from the build job
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-output
          path: dist/
      - run: ls -la dist/
      - run: echo "Deploying build output..."

  # Download all artifacts
  collect:
    needs: [build, test]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        # Omit 'name' to download ALL artifacts
      - run: ls -la  # Each artifact in its own directory
```

## Download via gh CLI

```bash
# List artifacts for a run
gh run view 12345 --json artifacts --jq '.artifacts[].name'

# Download a specific artifact from the latest run
gh run download --name build-output

# Download from a specific run
gh run download 12345 --name build-output

# Download all artifacts from a run
gh run download 12345

# Download to a specific directory
gh run download 12345 --name build-output --dir ./downloads

# Download by pattern
gh run download 12345 --pattern "test-*"
```

## REST API

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";
const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// List artifacts for a repo
const { data: artifacts } = await octokit.actions.listArtifactsForRepo({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  per_page: 30,
});
artifacts.artifacts.forEach(a => {
  console.log(a.name, a.size_in_bytes, a.expires_at);
});

// List artifacts for a specific run
const { data: runArtifacts } = await octokit.actions.listWorkflowRunArtifacts({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  run_id: 12345,
});

// Download an artifact (returns redirect to zip)
const { url } = await octokit.actions.downloadArtifact({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  artifact_id: 67890,
  archive_format: "zip",
});

// Delete an artifact
await octokit.actions.deleteArtifact({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  artifact_id: 67890,
});
```

## Retention and Limits

| Setting | Default | Configurable |
|---------|---------|---------------|
| Retention period | 90 days | 1-400 days (per artifact or repo setting) |
| Max artifact size | 500 MB | N/A |
| Max total storage | Varies by plan | Free: 500 MB, Pro: 1 GB, Enterprise: 50 GB |
| Compression | Automatic (zip) | N/A |

Set retention per artifact:
```yaml
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: logs
    path: "*.log"
    retention-days: 5
```

Set repo-level default via Settings > Actions > General > Artifact and log retention.

sections.title: Uploading artifacts
sections.body: Use `actions/upload-artifact@v4` in workflow steps. Specify `name` (artifact name), `path` (files/directories to upload), and optional `retention-days`. Use glob patterns and multi-line `path` for multiple files. Set `if-no-files-found: error` to fail if no files match. Use `if: always()` to upload even when previous steps fail (useful for test reports).

sections.title: Downloading between jobs
sections.body: Use `actions/download-artifact@v4` in a job that `needs:` the uploading job. Specify `name` to download a specific artifact or omit to download all. Each artifact extracts to a directory matching its name. Artifacts are available to all jobs in the same workflow run.

sections.title: Downloading externally
sections.body: Use `gh run download` to download artifacts from the CLI. Use `octokit.actions.downloadArtifact()` for API access — it returns a redirect URL to a zip archive. List artifacts with `listWorkflowRunArtifacts()`. Artifacts require authentication to download. Expired artifacts return 410 Gone.

sections.title: Retention and storage
sections.body: Default retention is 90 days. Override per-artifact with `retention-days` or set a repo-level default. Expired artifacts are automatically deleted. Monitor storage usage in repo Settings > Actions. Delete artifacts early with `octokit.actions.deleteArtifact()` to free storage. Artifact storage counts against your plan's quota.

links.context: Rate limits for artifact API calls

Artifacts are uploaded/downloaded in workflow steps

scripts.name: Download Artifacts
scripts.description: Download artifacts from a workflow run using gh CLI
